title: Image Format Converter
description: Convert PNG, JPG, WebP, BMP, and HEIC to any format. Batch convert with ZIP download — all in your browser.

privacy: All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your images are never uploaded to any server.

howToUse.label: Select output format
howToUse.description: Choose the target format: JPEG, PNG, or WebP.

howToUse.label: Add images
howToUse.description: Drag and drop images onto the drop zone, or click to browse. Supports PNG, JPG, WebP, BMP, and HEIC.

howToUse.label: Convert
howToUse.description: Click the Convert button to process all files in your browser.

howToUse.label: Download
howToUse.description: Download each file individually, or click "Download ZIP" to get all converted images in a single archive.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Input formats: PNG, JPG/JPEG, WebP, BMP, HEIC, and HEIF. Output formats: JPEG, PNG, and WebP.

faq.question: Can I convert HEIC files from iPhone?
faq.answer: Yes. HEIC and HEIF files (used on iPhone and newer Apple devices) can be converted to JPEG, PNG, or WebP directly in your browser.

faq.question: Are my images uploaded to a server?
faq.answer: No. All conversion happens entirely in your browser using the Canvas API. Your images never leave your device.

faq.question: How many files can I convert at once?
faq.answer: There is no strict limit, but performance depends on your device. For best results, convert in batches of 20 files or fewer.

faq.question: Does converting to JPEG reduce quality?
faq.answer: JPEG is a lossy format, so some quality loss occurs. This tool uses 92% quality, which provides a good balance between file size and visual fidelity. PNG and WebP conversions do not lose quality.
